what_it_does: This file is a channel attachment metadata record that links an attachment identifier to a board item in the dragnet-development channel.
observations: The file consists solely of YAML frontmatter with no body content.
The target is a board URI 'board://item-d4360679'.
It records an ISO-8601 added timestamp '2026-06-24T01:32:20.955820+00:00'.
standouts: The target uses a board:// URI scheme to reference a board item.
The file is purely a linkage/provenance stub with no payload.
It is typed specifically as a board_items attachment.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata.
Fields include id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added.
References external resources with a URI-style target.
concerns: No attachment payload or description is included in the file.
The board:// target cannot be resolved or verified from this file alone.
No author or modification history is recorded.
notes: A timestamped metadata stub that records a board_items attachment pointing to a specific board item in the dragnet-development channel.
questions: What content or artifact is actually attached via this record?
Who created or added this attachment?
What does board://item-d4360679 represent, and where is its definition stored?
Is there a separate payload file associated with this attachment stub?
purpose_vs_actual: The declared purpose as a board_items attachment matches what the file does—it records a link to a board item—but it contains only metadata and no actual attachment content.
intention_signals: To create a durable, timestamped record associating a channel attachment with a specific board item for later retrieval, audit, or cross-referencing.
novelty: It uses a board:// URI as the target value for an attachment reference.
contribution: Provides a minimal, timestamped linkage between a channel attachment identifier and a board item, supporting audit and cross-reference without embedding payload data.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a YAML-frontmatter channel attachment record with id att-9a235df7 in the dragnet-development channel, typed as board_items, targeting board://item-d4360679 and recording an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to persist a reference from a channel attachment to a specific board item for tracking or retrieval.
